findings.reachability: Any code that hooks the 'dgwt/wcas/search_page/result_post_ids' filter (another plugin, theme, or mu-plugin) can inject arbitrary values; the filter result is also indirectly influenced by the attacker-controlled search term processed in set_search_post_ids_from_ajax.
findings.justification: At line 45, $post_ids (obtained via apply_filters) is concatenated directly into a SQL fragment with implode(',', $post_ids) and no intval() cast, integer validation, or $wpdb->prepare() — any non-integer value injected through that filter becomes raw SQL.

detection_pattern.section_name: SQL Injection: implode into raw SQL fragment
detection_pattern.description: Catches implode() calls whose result is concatenated into a SQL string touching $wpdb without prepare() or integer casting. Non-integer values produce second-order or filter-supplied SQL injection.

findings.reachability: Any unauthenticated or authenticated user can POST to admin-ajax.php?action=filtersFrontend; no nonce is checked (the phpcs:ignore at line 51 explicitly suppresses the warning).
findings.justification: Both wp_ajax_filtersFrontend and wp_ajax_nopriv_filtersFrontend are registered without any nonce verification, allowing cross-site or direct requests to trigger the search engine query using an attacker-supplied URL string.

detection_pattern.section_name: CSRF: nopriv AJAX handler without nonce
detection_pattern.description: Detects unauthenticated AJAX handlers that perform state-changing or information-leaking operations without nonce verification; files matching the first pattern but not the second are suspicious.

findings.reachability: Any unauthenticated visitor (nopriv handler) who can POST to admin-ajax.php?action=filtersFrontend with a crafted 'currenturl' value.
findings.justification: The search term is extracted from $_POST['currenturl'] via wp_parse_url() and wp_parse_str() (lines 63-68) with no sanitization — wc_clean() is only applied to 'orderby' and 'order', not to 's' — before being passed verbatim to Helpers::searchProducts() at line 82.

detection_pattern.section_name: Missing sanitization: parsed URL query values used without cleaning
detection_pattern.description: Catches wp_parse_str() usage where extracted array values are not sanitized before being forwarded to downstream functions; unvalidated user-supplied query parameters can propagate into search or SQL contexts.

findings.reachability: Unauthenticated users via the nopriv AJAX action.
findings.justification: The wp_ajax_nopriv_filtersFrontend handler performs a live search-engine query on behalf of any unauthenticated caller without rate limiting, capability check, or nonce, enabling unlimited unauthenticated search-index probing.

detection_pattern.section_name: Broken Access Control: nopriv handler without capability check
detection_pattern.description: Detects AJAX handlers available to unauthenticated users that perform privileged or information-sensitive operations without any authorization check.
